summary.intent: Add a skip mechanism to local-validate.sh so that re-running it for an already-validated commit exits immediately instead of repeating the full validation.
summary.outcome: Implemented a SHA-based marker file (.local-validate-ok) that records successful validation; subsequent runs for the same commit short-circuit with an 'already validated' message. The marker file was gitignored, and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a script at scripts/local-validate.sh for local pre-commit/PR validation.
.gitignore already existed and was extended to include .local-validate-ok.

code.finding: SHA-based skip logic was added: reads current commit SHA, compares against .local-validate-ok marker file, exits 0 if matched, writes SHA to marker on successful completion.
Added .local-validate-ok to prevent the validation marker file from being tracked.

workflow: Using a temporary marker file keyed by commit SHA is an effective pattern for making idempotent local validation scripts.
The marker file approach avoids CI/remote state and works entirely locally.

open_items: The marker file approach only tracks one commit at a time; if the user switches branches and returns, the old SHA check may be stale or require a manual reset.
No mechanism to force-rerun validation on the same commit without deleting .local-validate-ok manually.
